meaning: n.呻吟,叹息vi.呻吟,受折磨,受压迫发出嘎吱声vt.呻吟着说
examples: Dorothea let out a histrionic groan.
She heard him let out a pitiful, muffled groan.
They glanced at the man on the floor, who began to groan.
The sport makes me want to cheer, sometimes groan, and always analyze the results.
Most real estate owners groan when told they must keep a detailed log of their time.
